items.title: Firefighters tackle blaze at Geelong oil refinery 'not yet under control'

children.content: Authorities were called to one of Australia's two operational oil refineries around 11pm after residents reported "explosions and flames".

items.title: Albanese government's frustration rises as Australians pay price for war

children.content: Fuel prices and inflation aren't the only metrics climbing as the Strait of Hormuz remains shut. The Albanese government's level of frustration is rising just as fast.

items.title: Creators of fuel dashboard quoted by News Corp have links to Liberals

children.content: An ABC NEWS Verify investigation raises questions about popular independent fuel security dashboards that have emerged online, including one whose creators are linked to the Liberal Party.

image.altText: A graph showing a plane, a fuel pump and a plane with a countdown in red.

children.content: The US president has said a deal with Iran is "preferable" and to expect an "amazing two days" ahead. Follow live.

items.title: Ukraine calls for more aid as Russia continues daily drone strikes

children.content: Western officials and analysts say Ukrainian forces have disrupted Russia's planned spring offensive and managed to retake territory.

image.altText: A man standing by the ruins of a bombed out car in a car park.

items.title: At least nine dead, including eight children, in Türkiye school shooting

children.content: A boy, believed to be aged about 13, carried out the shooting in southeastern Türkiye, killing his fellow students and a teacher. It is the second school shooting in the country in two days.

image.altText: turkiye school shooting

items.title: Dozens of workers with disability made redundant by award-winning charity

children.content: Help Enterprises made 41 positions redundant weeks after being named Brisbane's social enterprise of the year.

items.title: Taylor's migration plan doesn't make much sense. It doesn't have to

children.content: More than a day after Angus Taylor unveiled the first plank of the Coalition's long-awaited migration policy and many media interviews later, it's still unclear what the opposition is actually proposing to change.

image.altText: A man in a suit extends his hand in exclamation while speaking to a group of journalists.

items.title: 'Canary in coal mine' warning as marine pest found in new area

children.content: The discovery on Tasmania's west coast has scientists and fishers alike fearing the spread of a destructive species to another previously untouched part of the state's marine environment.

items.title: Short-stay levy overperforms, as holiday rental growth stagnates in Victoria

children.content: Data suggests the growth in short-stay accommodation listings in Victoria has slowed in the year after the Victorian government's new tax came into effect.

items.title: Councils become insurers as premiums rise by 500pc

children.content: Adam Osborne was quoted a staggering $60,000 for his home insurance this year. With others in the same boat, six Queensland councils are looking at creating their own insurance mutual to drive down premiums.

image.altText: image of a phone with a bill for $60k superimposed over a river

items.title: Defence spending to climb by $53 billion as 'international norms' erode

children.content: Australia's defence spending will climb by $53 billion over the next decade amid what the federal government says are "intensifying" global risks.

image.altText: Richard Marles

items.title: American YouTuber jailed in South Korea for being a public nuisance

children.content: A Seoul court found Johnny Somali displayed “severe” disrespect for South Korean law over a series of provocative stunts he live-streamed.

items.title: How this tiny town welcomed a 70-piece orchestra

children.content: The Sydney Youth Orchestras travelled to this tiny town to help inspire the local kids, but when they left, they were the ones who felt changed.

items.title: How the technology we use every day is changing our brains

children.content: If it feels harder to concentrate than it used to, it's not just you. Scientists say your brain is reacting exactly as it was designed to — but in a very different world.

items.title: Teen admits creating deepfakes in Australian-first prosecution

children.content: An Adelaide teenager who was the first person charged under new nationwide laws aimed at tackling adult deepfake pornography has pleaded guilty to two counts of creating or altering sexual material without consent.

image.altText: William Yeates is wearing a white shirt and looking over his shoulder

items.title: Media boss's wife feared she would die, tore his clothing during alleged attack, court told

children.content: The wife of media executive Antony Catalano fled the couple's luxury apartment and flagged down a passing car for help following a sustained attack by her husband that caused hospitalisation, police allege.

image.altText: A side profile shot of Australian Community Media executive chairman Antony Catalano in an outdoor setting.

items.title: Here's what you need to know about the Gina Rinehart court ruling

children.content: The blockbuster court ruling over mining tenements worth billions of dollars in WA's iron ore rich Pilbara is the culmination of a complicated legal battle spanning more than a decade that has pitted family against family and siblings against each other.

articles.title: Split decision as Gina Rinehart, Wright family both claim legal wins

children.content: Gina Rinehart is dealt a blow, with a court finding the family of her father's former business partner has the right to potentially hundreds of millions of dollars in royalties from lucrative Pilbara mining assets.

image.altText: A woman in a black jacket and pink shirt steps on stage

items.title: Cage fighter was 'mucking around' when spar turned deadly, jury hears

children.content: Jurors have heard that the accused's girlfriend suggested he call an ambulance after the sparring session, but he told her to "hurry up and just get here".

image.altText: a forensic officer wearing a hazmat suit takes pictures at a mount druitt home after an alleged murder

items.title: Power confirms appeal after reasons released for Butters AFL Tribunal decision

children.content: The AFL Tribunal releases its reasons for finding Zak Butters guilty of abusive and insulting language to umpire Nick Foot, saying it was "satisfied to the requisite standard" that he had made the remarks and that it was "implausible" that the umpire would have made it up.

image.altText: A Port Adelaide AFL player (middle) stands in the rain staring up field with a concerned look on his face.

items.title: Brunei's fertiliser could feed to Australia following meeting of leaders

children.content: Brunei may be able to help Australia weather the economic chaos caused by the Iran war by sending additional supplies of fertiliser, the prime minister says.

image.altText: Two men in suits holding booklets stand in front of gold chairs.

items.title: Lesbian Action Group wins appeal in case seeking to ban trans women

children.content: The Federal Court allows a lesbian action group's appeal to be allowed to legally exclude transgender women from its public events.

image.altText: Lesbian Action Group celebrate the win outside court

items.title: How The Babadook inspired the 'scariest movie you'll ever hear'

items.title: Jury hears 'no bad blood' before sistergirl's alleged fatal stabbing

children.content: Jurors in the murder trial of a man accused of fatally stabbing a 37-year-old sistergirl from the Tiwi Islands have heard there was "no bad blood" between them in the lead-up to the "family tragedy".

items.title: Brothers living in squalor missed multiple health appointments, report finds

children.content: A 48-page report has been released on the interactions between health care workers and two siblings who were discovered living in squalor in May 2020.
